response_constraints: Base the final answer only on returned tool results; do not imply that a skipped or failed call succeeded.
Respect the stage boundaries: calls grouped as parallel must be issued together in one assistant action, and later calls must wait for the IDs, status, date, or mutation result they consume.
Do not use extra write or notification tools beyond the single action path explicitly requested.

stages.purpose: Resolve the two independent names in one parallel search action.
Retrieve the unique stage-1 matches together before checking the mutation condition.
Update Refund status case only after stage 2 verifies its unique ID and pending-customer status.

arguments.body: Refund status case was updated to resolved after status verification.
arguments.subject: Record update: Refund status case

stages.purpose: Notify the named operational recipient only after stage 3 returns a successful mutation.

state_assertions: Record sup-685 changes from pending-customer to resolved.
Record sup-285 and distractor sup-1085 remain unchanged.
Exactly one notification is sent to support lead, and only after the mutation succeeds.

prompt: Resolve Broken link case in Acme Cooperative and Refund status case in Beacon Arts with parallel searches, then retrieve both unique matches together. If Refund status case is still pending-customer, change only Refund status case from pending-customer to resolved. Leave Broken link case unchanged. Only after that mutation succeeds, notify support lead with the outcome; do not send a notice if the mutation is skipped or fails. Do not take any extra write or notification action beyond the path I specified.

stage_4.body: Refund status case was updated to resolved after status verification.
